response: The internet’s roots trace to the 1960s, when the US Department of Defense funded computer networking research that led to ARPANET, first demonstrated in 1969 with a message sent from UCLA to Stanford Research Institute. Key milestones include the 1983 switch to TCP/IP, Tim Berners-Lee’s invention of the World Wide Web in 1989 (with HTML, URLs, and HTTP), and the 1993 launch of the Mosaic browser that helped make the web widely accessible. After the late-1990s dot-com boom and 2001 crash, major survivors like Amazon and Google emerged, while social media (mid-2000s) and mobile internet (after the 2007 iPhone) further expanded usage; cloud computing took off with AWS in 2006. Today, the internet connects over 5 billion people and supports nearly all areas of modern life, including commerce, communication, and entertainment.
